24 February 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • US military can sustain only days of attacks against Iran: Israeli intel. Press TV 24 Feb 2026 -- Israeli intelligence officials estimate that the United States could sustain only "four to five days" of military strikes against Iran if it were to launch acts of aggression against the Islamic Republic, a new report reveals.

  • Venezuela calls on UN to pressure US for Maduro's immediate release weeks after abduction Press TV 24 Feb 2026 -- Venezuela has formally petitioned the United Nations for the immediate release of President Nicolas Maduro, following his abduction by US forces in an early January operation that Caracas slammed as "illegal."

  • Coast Guard and Department of Homeland Security partner agencies interdict multiple suspected smuggling vessels south of San Clemente Island US Coast Guard 24 Feb 2026 -- The U.S. Coast Guard and Department of Homeland Security partner agencies, supported by the U.S. Navy, interdicted five suspected maritime smuggling vessels and apprehended 62 aliens during coordinated operations south of San Clemente Island, Saturday.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Rogers on the 2026 State of the Union Address House Armed Services Committee 24 Feb 2026

  • Trump says top general to lead potential strike on Iran "if ordered" People's Daily 24 Feb 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday that Dan Caine, the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, will lead a potential strike on Iran if he is ordered to do so.
  • Trump's top general warns of Iran aggression risks: reports Press TV 24 Feb 2026 -- US media report that General Dan Caine, Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, has warned President Donald Trump against the myriad dangers associated with acts of aggression against Iran, including the high probability of a costly, long-term entanglement.

  • Smith Statement on ROTOR Act House Armed Services Committee - Democrats 24 Feb 2026 -- U.S. Representative Adam Smith (D-Wash.), Ranking Member of the House Armed Services Committee, released the following statement about the failed vote for the ROTOR Act on the floor of the House of Representatives.
  • Securing Rare Earth Elements a National Security Imperative, Official Says DoD News 24 Feb 2026 -- Securing a resilient supply chain for critical minerals is fundamental to national security and the economy, said Michael P. Cadenazzi Jr., assistant secretary of war for industrial base policy, who testified today at a Senate Armed Services Committee hearing in Washington.
  • Joint Interagency Task Force Announces Counter-UAS Marketplace DoD News 24 Feb 2026 -- Joint Interagency Task Force 401 announced today that its revolutionary counter-unmanned aircraft systems marketplace has reached initial operational capability.
  • Justified Accord 2026 Begins in Kenya, Tanzania DoD News 24 Feb 2026 -- Exercise Justified Accord 2026 the U.S. Africa Command's largest annual, multinational exercise in East Africa, officially began yesterday across Djibouti, Kenya and Tanzania.
  • Air Force Secretary Outlines Priorities DoD News 24 Feb 2026 -- The Department of the Air Force's priorities are readiness, modernization, defending the homeland — including the entire hemisphere, border security, missile defense, nuclear deterrence and people, said Air Force Secretary Troy Meink, who spoke yesterday during the Air and Space Forces Association's 2026 Warfare Symposium in Aurora, Colorado.
  • Justified Accord 2026 begins in Kenya, Tanzania, Djibouti Army News 24 Feb 2026 -- Exercise Justified Accord 2026 (JA26), U.S. Africa Command's largest annual, multinational exercise in East Africa, officially began Feb. 24 across Djibouti, Kenya and Tanzania. Led by U.S. Army Southern European Task Force, Africa (SETAF-AF), JA26 integrates approximately 1,500 personnel from Djibouti, Kenya, Tanzania the United States and several other nations to build readiness, deepen partnerships and strengthen regional security.
  • U.S. security force assistance brigade trains Djiboutian rapid intervention force Army News 24 Feb 2026 -- U.S. Soldiers assigned to Logistics Company Advising Team 6640 of 6th Battalion, 54th Security Force Assistance Brigade, trained 80 members of Djibouti's 2nd Company, Bataillon d'Intervention Rapide (Rapid Intervention Battalion) in night range operations on Goubet Range, Djibouti, Oct. 15-Nov. 25, 2025.
  • SECAF opens 2026 AFA Warfare Symposium with 'Innovating Faster: Acquisition Transformation' US Air Force 24 Feb 2026 -- After nearly 10 months on the job, Secretary of the Air Force Troy Meink was back in front of a crowd of Airmen, Guardians, allies, partners and industry leaders, excited to deliver his keynote at the 2026 Air and Space Forces Association Warfare Symposium, Feb. 23.
  • Wilsbach outlines strategy for confronting adversaries, Air Force success US Air Force 24 Feb 2026 -- In his first major address since becoming Air Force Chief of Staff, Gen. Ken Wilsbach used personal examples of stellar performances from pilots, maintainers, health care workers and others to highlight readiness and how the service is responding to a strategic environment that is "complex and ambiguous."
  • Pentagon to Spend $4.5Bln to Accelerate Bomber Production - Air Force Sputnik 24 Feb 2026 -- A $4.5 billion contract to increase B-21 Raider bomber production capacity has been awarded to Northrop Grumman, the US Air Force said.
  • Coast Guard Reinstates 56 Members Who Were Dismissed for Refusing COVID Vaccines Under Biden Administration US Coast Guard 24 Feb 2026 -- The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) announced Monday that 56 members of the United States Coast Guard who were kicked out of the service over the COVID-19 vaccine have finally been reinstated with back pay.
  • Musk's Grok of xAI signs deal with Pentagon, becomes military's new partner in classified systems Press TV 24 Feb 2026 -- In a stark display of expanding militarization of artificial intelligence, Elon Musk's xAI has made a deal with the Pentagon to deploy its Grok model inside classified military systems as US military officials threaten rivals who refuse to loosen safeguards.
  • Aligning air, space power: Operational perspectives at the AFA Warfare Symposium United States Space Force 24 Feb 2026 -- As the character of warfare continues to evolve, leaders from Buckley Space Force Base underscored the operational importance of integrating air and spacepower during a panel discussion at the 2026 Air and Space Forces Association Warfare Symposium in Aurora, Feb. 23.
  • Saltzman details focus on speed, accepting risk, long-term force design United States Space Force 24 Feb 2026 -- Chief of Space Operations Gen. Chance Saltzman said, Feb. 23, the U.S. Space Force must accelerate how it builds and fields combat capability to maintain advantage in an increasingly contested space domain.

  • Greenland leader rejects Trump's idea of sending U.S. hospital ship People's Daily 24 Feb 2026 -- Greenlandic Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen said on Sunday that the autonomous territory of Denmark is not interested in receiving a U.S. hospital ship that U.S. President Donald Trump wants to send to the island.

  • NATO's safe skies, Allies conduct drone training mission on Eastern Flank NATO Allied Air Command 24 Feb 2026 -- On 20 February 2026, NATO Allied Air Command (AIRCOM) conducted counter Unmanned Aircraft System (c-UAS) training over the Baltics to reinforce Allied deterrence, strengthen cohesion across the Alliance, and enhance operational readiness along NATO's eastern flank, in support of NATO's enhanced Vigilance Activity, Eastern Sentry (EASN), and Joint Force Command Brunssum's (JFC-Brunssum) premier exercise, Steadfast Dart 26.
  • NATO maritime groups reinforce deterrence during major exercise Steadfast Dart 26 NATO Allied Maritime Command 24 Feb 2026 -- NATO's premier joint deployment exercise for 2026 has drawn to a close. Hosted by Germany under NATO's Joint Force Command Brunssum, Exercise Steadfast Dart 26 involved 10,000 troops from 13 Allied nations, showcasing NATO's ability to rapidly deploy and employ the Allied Reaction Force (ARF) in a crisis scenario across land, sea, air, space, and cyber domains.

  • Recruited In Russia, Kyrgyz Men Go Missing In Ukraine -- Leaving Wives Desperate RFE/RL 24 Feb 2026 -- Kyrgyz citizen Ernisbek Mamasydykov did not expect to get caught up in Russia's invasion of Ukraine, but one day in August last year he found himself in a military uniform headed for the front.

  • Denmark to finance infrastructure modernisation at an Armed Forces of Ukraine training centre Ukraine MOD 24 Feb 2026
  • Mykhailo Fedorov and the Minister of National Defence of Lithuania discuss security cooperation plans for 2026 Ukraine MOD 24 Feb 2026
  • What is known about the AASM Hammer guided aerial bombs provided by France for Ukrainian aircraft Ukraine MOD 24 Feb 2026
  • Support for Ukraine's Air Defense, Energy and Resilience, Stronger Sanctions Against Russia, Negotiations, and Security Guarantees - Key Topics of the Coalition of the Willing Meeting President of Ukraine 24 Feb 2026
  • Only the Help of Our Friends and the Resilience of Our People Gave Us the Possibility to Survive - Address by the President to the Participants of the Coalition of the Willing President of Ukraine 24 Feb 2026
  • UK steps up support for Ukraine four years on from Putin's full-scale invasion UK MOD 24 Feb 2026 -- UK boosting support for Ukraine four years after Putin's full-scale invasion with new military and humanitarian support.
  • Danish contribution strengthens the training of Ukrainian soldiers Danish Ministry of Defence 24 Feb 2026 -- Denmark is contributing to a new initiative that will strengthen and modernise training efforts for the Armed Forces of Ukraine. Minister for Defence Troels Lund Poulsen presented the contribution today in Kyiv together with his Ukrainian counterpart and the Ukrainian organisation Come Back Alive
  • Written message from Prime Minister TAKAICHI for Virtual Leaders Meeting of the Coalition of the Willing on Ukraine Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 24 Feb 2026
  • We provide critically needed missiles, says Minister R. Kaunas after meeting with V.Zelenskyy in Kyiv Lithuania MND 24 Feb 2026 -- Minister of National Defence Robertas Kaunas on a visit to Ukraine for commemoration of the 4th anniversary of the wide-scale war attended meetings of the Coalition of the Willing and The Nordic-Baltic Eight (NB8) on Tuesday.

  • US hypes China's nuclear arsenal buildup at Conference on Disarmament; experts call US tactic a pretext for own nuclear expansion Global Times 24 Feb 2026 -- "China firmly supports the purpose and objectives of the Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty, and the US accusations that China is conducting nuclear explosive tests are entirely groundless and are merely an excuse fabricated to justify its own potential resumption of nuclear testing," Shen Jian, Chinese ambassador for disarmament affairs, said at a high-level meeting on disarmament in Geneva on Monday local time.
  • Chinese FM responds to whether China and US will hold talks on arms control Global Times 24 Feb 2026 -- In response to a question about reports that China will hold talks with the US in Geneva on Tuesday local time as the US and Russia are in talks in Geneva over arms control, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said at a press conference on Tuesday that during the high-level segment of the Conference on Disarmament in Geneva this week, the Chinese delegation is willing to maintain communication with participating delegations of various countries and exchange views on the work of the Conference on Disarmament as well as on issues related to the Review Conference of the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons.

  • Ministry of National Defense: Minister Wellington Koo Li-hsiung Urges Legislative Yuan to Authorize Signing of Three Arms Sales LOAs Before March 15 Deadline ROC MND 24 Feb 2026
  • Political parties agree to send special defense bill to committee CNA 24 Feb 2026 -- Ruling and opposition parties agreed Tuesday to place the Executive Yuan's draft special act on defense procurement on the Legislative Yuan's March 6 plenary agenda and refer it to committee for review.

  • $250 million upgrade secures Navy's future in North Queensland Australia DOD 24 Feb 2026 -- The Albanese Government is strengthening Defence capability in northern Australia, with major milestones reached on delivering the $250 million HMAS Cairns wharf upgrade and the completion of a new shared-user training facility for Australian Navy Cadets.
  • Albanese Government announces acquisition of long-lead items for AUKUS nuclear propulsion systems Australia DOD 24 Feb 2026 -- The Albanese Government has announced a $310 million payment for the acquisition of long-lead items from the United Kingdom to support Australia's future sovereign conventionally-armed, nuclear-powered submarine capability.
  • Australia's steadfast support for Ukraine four years on Australia DOD 24 Feb 2026 -- "Today marks four years since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine began in blatant violation of international law, including the United Nations Charter."
  • New landing craft to boost littoral defence Australia DOD 24 Feb 2026 -- Army's ability to fight and win in the littoral is one step closer with the signing of a $4 billion agreement for the construction of eight landing craft heavy (LCH) vessels.
  • Trans-Tasman teamwork strengthens care Australia DOD 25 Feb 2026 -- Exercise Trojan Compass has brought a flurry of activity to RAAF Base Richmond, with aircraft movements and medical teams working at a heightened tempo.

  • US accusations of China conducting nuclear tests entirely groundless, an excuse to justify own potential resumption of testing: Chinese envoy Global Times 24 Feb 2026 -- China firmly supports the purpose and objectives of the Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty, and the US accusations that China is conducting nuclear explosive tests are entirely groundless and are merely an excuse fabricated to justify its own potential resumption of nuclear testing, Shen Jian, Chinese ambassador for disarmament affairs, said at a high-level meeting on disarmament in Geneva on Monday local time, according to the China Central Television (CCTV).
  • Application scenarios, more details of China's first unmanned cargo aircraft 'Tianma-1000' unveiled Global Times 24 Feb 2026 -- China Central Television unveiled application scenarios and released more details of China's first unmanned cargo aircraft - the "Tianma-1000" on Tuesday, noting that the aircraft is capable of adapting to high-altitude complex terrain, achieving ultra-short takeoff and landing, and swiftly switching between cargo transport and airdrop mode.

  • INDIA-US JOINT SPECIAL FORCES EXERCISE VAJRA PRAHAR TO BE CONDUCTED IN HIMACHAL PRADESH India PIB 24 Feb 2026 -- The US Army contingent has arrived in India to participate in the 16th edition of the India-US Joint Special Forces Exercise VAJRA PRAHAR, scheduled to be conducted at the Special Forces Training School, Bakloh, Himachal Pradesh, from 24 February to 16 March 2026
  • INDIA-JAPAN JOINT EXERCISE 'DHARMA GUARDIAN' COMMENCES IN CHAUBATTIA, UTTARAKHAND India PIB 24 Feb 2026 -- The 7th edition of the annual Joint Military Exercise 'DHARMA GUARDIAN' between the Indian Army and the Japan Ground Self-Defense Force (JGSDF) commenced today at the Foreign Training Node, Chaubattia in Uttarakhand

  • Iran ready to expand military cooperation with Armenia: Top general IRNA 24 Feb 2026 -- Chairman of the Chief of Staff of Iran's Armed Forces, Major General Abdolrahim Mousavi, has emphasized Iran's readiness to expand military cooperation with the Armed Forces of Armenia.
  • Iran determined to finalize comprehensive strategic partnership document with Armenia: Pezeshkian IRNA 24 Feb 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian has announced the Islamic Republic's readiness to finalize a comprehensive strategic partnership document with Armenia, stressing that expanding bilateral ties would serve the interests of both nations.
  • IRGC Ground Forces showcase advanced weaponry in drills along Persian Gulf coasts Press TV 24 Feb 2026 -- The Ground Forces of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) have successfully concluded the large-scale Combined 1404 military exercises across Iran's southern coastlines and strategic islands.

  • Powerful new Royal Navy warship programme advances as milestones reached for two ships UK MOD 24 Feb 2026 -- Two of the Royal Navy's powerful new Type 31 frigates have reached key milestones.

  • EUMC Mini Away Day- ASPIDES Contributing to EU Maritime Security Challenges and Opportunities European External Action Service (EEAS) 24 Feb 2026 -- Captain Vasileios Tsiouflias (EL, N), Chief of Staff of the European Union Naval Force (EUNAVFOR) Operation ASPIDES, participated on a workshop, addressing coherent and effective EU maritime engagement and security.

  • Speech by Commander of the Estonian Defence Forces LTG Andrus Merilo Estonia Defence Forces 24 Feb 2026

  • Statement by Minister of Foreign Affairs of Armenia Ararat Mirzoyan at the High-Level Segment of the Conference on Disarmament Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Armenia 24 Feb 2026 -- On February 23, in Geneva, Foreign Minister of Armenia Ararat Mirzoyan participated and delivered remarks at the high-level segment of the Conference on Disarmament.
  • The Minister of Defence of the Republic of Armenia met with the President of the Islamic Republic of Iran Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia 24 Feb 2026
  • Suren Papikyan, met with the Minister of Defence and Armed Forces Logistics of the Islamic Republic of Iran Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia 24 Feb 2026
  • Suren Papikyan met with the Chief of the General Staff of the Iranian Armed Forces Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia 24 Feb 2026

  • AFP eyes expanding 'Balikatan' to include different missile systems Philippine News Agency 24 Feb 2026 -- A ranking naval official on Tuesday said the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) is looking to expand the different activities in the annual military exercises with the Americans to allow Filipino troops to "train in a multi-domain environment", including different missile systems.
  • Senate starts hearing measures strengthening PH defense institutions Philippine News Agency 24 Feb 2026 -- The Senate on Tuesday began deliberations on measures aimed at strengthening and modernizing the country's defense institutions to address evolving security threats.
  • Navy welcomes PBBM order for more infra in Kalayaan Island Group Philippine News Agency 24 Feb 2026 -- A ranking Philippine Navy (PN) official on Tuesday welcomed President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr.'s calls to provide more infrastructure for Pag-asa Island, the largest Philippine-occupied feature in the Kalayaan Island Group (KIG).

  • UNRWA Situation Report #209 on the humanitarian crisis in the Gaza Strip and the occupied West Bank, including East Jerusalem UNRWA 24 Feb 2026

  • Hamas: Settler attack on West Bank mosque 'a fascist and organized crime' IRNA 24 Feb 2026 -- The Palestinian resistance movement, Hamas, has condemned Israeli settlers for setting fire to a mosque in the occupied West Bank, describing the act as a "fascist and organized crime."
  • Hamas denounces Israel's threats to destroy resistance, slams ceasefire violations Press TV 24 Feb 2026 -- Hamas has condemned Israel's repeated threats to eliminate the resistance movement, stating that ongoing violations of the Gaza ceasefire reflect a complete disregard for international agreements and diplomatic efforts aimed at halting the genocide.
  • Hamas condemns Israel's arson attack on mosque in West Bank, calls for mobilization Press TV 24 Feb 2026 -- The Palestinian resistance movement Hamas has condemned an Israeli arson attack on the Abu Bakr as-Siddiq Mosque in the village of Tal, west of Nablus, describing the incident as a "fascist crime" and a violation of international protections for religious sites.

  • Lebanese army orders troops to return fire after Israeli attack near border post Press TV 24 Feb 2026 -- Lebanon's army says it has ordered its forces to return fire after Israeli forces targeted an observation post under construction in the southern Marjayoun district.
  • Israel warns Lebanon of severe strikes if Hezbollah joins any US-Iran war: Report Press TV 24 Feb 2026 -- Israel has sent an indirect warning to Lebanon that it would conduct severe strikes on the country, including targeting civilian infrastructure such as Beirut's airport, if the resistance movement Hezbollah becomes involved in any potential war between the United States and Iran.

  • The OIC Media Observatory Issues its 117th Report, Documenting the Killing and Wounding of 54 Palestinians, the Demolition of 24 Homes, the Occupation of 4, and the Displacement of 15 Families Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 24 Feb 2026 -- Israeli occupation forces continued their crimes in the Gaza Strip and the West Bank, killing and wounding 54 Palestinians between February 17 and 23, 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, South Sudan, Ukraine, Syria, Madagascar UNOCHA 24 Feb 2026

  • Remarks by UNICEF Executive Director Catherine Russell at the United Nations Security Council arria-formula meeting for Red Hand Day 2026: Safe education to prevent recruitment and use of children in armed conflict UNICEF 24 Feb 2026

  • No Chinese military base in Scarborough Shoal - Navy exec Philippine News Agency 24 Feb 2026 -- A ranking Philippine Navy (PN) official on Tuesday denied claims that Chinese forces have constructed a military base inside Scarborough Shoal (also known as Bajo de Masinloc) in the West Philippine Sea (WPS).

  • OIC Condemns Terrorist Attacks in Zamfara State, North-western Nigeria Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 24 Feb 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has condemned in the strongest terms the heinous terrorist attacks committed on 21 February 2026 in Zamfara State, north-western Nigeria, which resulted in the killing of dozens of innocent civilians and the abduction of women and children

  • Prosecution alleges 'systematic' pattern of killings under Duterte Philippine News Agency 24 Feb 2026 -- The drug war in the Philippines under former president Rodrigo R. Duterte was "widespread and systematic," prosecutors alleged Tuesday before the International Criminal Court (ICC) in The Hague, the Netherlands.

  • UNHCR calls for urgent support to sustain refugee protection and returnee reintegration in Burundi UNHCR 24 Feb 2026 -- UNHCR, the UN Refugee Agency, is renewing its call for urgent funding to scale up lifesaving aid in Burundi, warning that the country is under intensifying humanitarian pressure as it hosts over 90,000 refugees who have escaped conflict in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) in the past three months.
  • Concerns raised about 'coercive' repatriation of Burundian refugees from Tanzania UN News 24 Feb 2026 -- Concerns have been raised about the "coercive" repatriation from Tanzania of Burundian refugees, many of whom do not want to return to their home country.

News Reports

  • Former General Manager for U.S. Defense Contractor Sentenced to 87 Months for Selling Stolen Trade Secrets to Russian Broker US Dept. of Justice 24 Feb 2026 -- Today, Peter Williams, 39, an Australian national, was sentenced in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia to 87 months in prison for selling his employer's trade secrets — sensitive and protected cyber-exploit components — to a Russian cyber-tools broker, announced the Department of Justice.

  • U.S. judge blocks release of report on Trump's classified documents case People's Daily 24 Feb 2026 -- A U.S. federal judge on Monday blocked the release of a report by former Special Counsel Jack Smith about President Donald Trump's alleged mishandling of classified documents following his first term.

  • National Terrorism Threat Level unchanged but described as POSSIBLE under updated language New Zealand Security Intelligence Service 24 Feb 2026 -- New Zealand's National Terrorism Threat Level (terrorism threat level) remains unchanged following an annual review by the Combined Threat Assessment Group (CTAG), an inter-agency group hosted and led by the New Zealand Security Intelligence Service (NZSIS).

  • Designation of Russia-Based Zero-Day Exploits Broker and Affiliates for Theft of U.S. Trade Secrets US Dept. of State 24 Feb 2026 -- Today, the Department of State announced the designations of one individual and two entities under the Protecting American Intellectual Property Act (PAIPA) in connection with theft of trade secrets from U.S. persons.
  • Treasury Sanctions Exploit Broker Network for Theft and Sale of U.S. Government Cyber Tools US Treasury 24 Feb 2026 -- Today, the Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) designated Sergey Sergeyevich Zelenyuk (Zelenyuk) and his company, Matrix LLC (doing business as Operation Zero), as well as five associated individuals and entities, for their acquisition and distribution of cyber tools harmful to U.S. national security.

  • Meeting of Federal Security Service Board President of Russia 24 Feb 2026 -- Vladimir Putin spoke at a meeting of the board of the Federal Security Service of the Russian Federation.
  • Meeting with Foreign Minister of Vietnam Le Hoai Trung President of Russia 24 Feb 2026 -- Vladimir Putin received Foreign Minister of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam Le Hoai Trung in the Kremlin.
  • Russia - Australia lowers price cap on Russian crude oil Australia DFAT 24 Feb 2026 -- On 24 February 2026 the Foreign Minister announced Australia had lowered the Oil Price Cap (OPC) applicable to maritime services provided in relation to Russian-origin crude oil from USD47.60 to USD44.10 per barrel.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Mao Ning's Regular Press Conference on February 24, 2026 PRC MofA 24 Feb 2026
  • Heads-of-state diplomacy plays an irreplaceable role in providing strategic guidance for China-US relations: FM responds to Trump's reported China trip Global Times 24 Feb 2026 -- When asked whether the news of an upcoming visit to China by US President Donald Trump can be confirmed, Chinese Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Mao Ning said on Tuesday that heads-of-state diplomacy plays an irreplaceable role in providing strategic guidance for China-US relations. "The Chinese and US sides are maintaining communication regarding President Trump's visit to China. However, regarding the specific question you raised, I have no information to offer at this time," Mao said.
  • China assessing impact of U.S. Supreme Court's tariffs ruling: spokesperson People's Daily 24 Feb 2026 -- China's Ministry of Commerce said on Monday that it has noted the U.S. Supreme Court's ruling against the U.S. government's tariffs imposed under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA), and is conducting a comprehensive assessment of its relevant content and impact.
  • China's LandSpace plans more recovery test launches of its Zhuque-3 reusable rocket in Q2 of 2026: company Global Times 25 Feb 2026 -- Chinese privately-owned space company LandSpace said at a recent meeting of the United Nations Committee on the Peaceful Uses of Outer Space that the company plans to conduct another recovery test of its Zhuque-3 reusable rocket in the second quarter of this year.

  • Trade deal's Section 232 provisions intact despite U.S. ruling: Taiwan CNA 24 Feb 2026 -- The recent ruling declaring President Donald Trump's "reciprocal" tariffs unconstitutional will not affect key favorable terms Taiwan obtained in a trade deal recently signed with the United States, Vice Premier Cheng Li-chiun (鄭麗君) said Tuesday.
  • Lai reiterates 'status quo' at China-based Taiwanese event CNA 24 Feb 2026 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) on Tuesday reiterated his commitment to "maintaining the status quo" in cross-strait relations, at a Lunar New Year event in Taichung attended by China-based Taiwanese businesspeople.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 24 Feb 2026

  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Receives Greetings from Russian Party Leader KCNA 24 Feb 2026 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, received a message of greeting from D. Medvedev, president of the United Russia Party, on Feb. 23.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Receives Congratulations from General Secretary Xi Jinping KCNA 24 Feb 2026 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea, received a congratulatory message from Xi Jinping, general secretary of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China, on February 23.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Receives Congratulations from General Secretary of C.C., CPV KCNA 24 Feb 2026 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea, received a congratulatory message from To Lam, general secretary of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of Vietnam, on February 22.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Receives Congratulations from General Secretary of C.C., LPRP KCNA 24 Feb 2026 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea, received a congratulatory message from Thongloun Sisoulith, general secretary of the Central Committee of the Lao People's Revolutionary Party, on February 23.
  • Fifth-day Sitting of Ninth WPK Congress Comrade Kim Jong Un Makes Important Concluding Speech KCNA 24 Feb 2026 -- The delegates to the Ninth Congress of the Workers' Party of Korea elected the great Comrade Kim Jong Un to the top post of the dignified WPK again. With higher political enthusiasm, they are continuing their work, according to the schedule of the congress, full of confidence in victory and optimism about the bright future of the WPK, the eternal prosperity of the DPRK and the rosy future of Korean-style socialism.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un's Concluding Speech at Ninth WPK Congress KCNA 24 Feb 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un made an important concluding speech at the Ninth Congress of the Workers' Party of Korea.
  • First Enlarged Plenary Meeting of Ninth Central Committee of WPK Held KCNA 24 Feb 2026 -- The first enlarged plenary meeting of the Ninth Central Committee of the Workers' Party of Korea was held at the headquarters building of the Party Central Committee on February 23.
  • Press Release of First Plenary Meeting of Ninth Central Committee of WPK KCNA 24 Feb 2026

  • Focus on diplomacy will benefit all; war is a real gamble: Deputy FM IRNA 24 Feb 2026 -- The deputy foreign minister for political affairs has warned that any war against Iran would be uncontrollable and amount to "a real gamble," urging all sides to prioritize diplomacy over military escalation.
  • FM says historic opportunity at hand for unprecedented deal; warns Iran protects sovereignty at any cost Press TV 24 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Iran and the United States have a "historic opportunity" at hand to strike an "unprecedented agreement," while warning that the Islamic Republic would protect its sovereignty at any cost in the event of renewed American military adventurism.
  • 'Real gamble': Iranian negotiator warns US cannot contain war with Iran Press TV 24 Feb 2026 -- Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Majid Takht-Ravanchi has warned the United States against any act of aggression, saying a military strike would be a "real gamble" and that no one would be able to contain the ensuing war.
  • Iran opposes any geopolitical changes, foreign interference in region: Defense minister IRNA 24 Feb 2026 -- Iran's Minister of Defense, Brigadier General Aziz Nasirzadeh, has highlighted Tehran's stance against any geopolitical shifts in the region and any interference by external actors.
  • Iran rejects any shift in geopolitics of region, defense minister says Press TV 24 Feb 2026 -- Iran's Minister of Defense, Brigadier General Aziz Nasirzadeh, reiterated Tehran's firm opposition to foreign intervention and its unwavering commitment to safeguarding national sovereignty during a high-level meeting with Armenia's Minister of Defense, Suren Papikyan, in Tehran on Tuesday.
  • Escalation of Mideast tension does not serve any party's interest: Chinese FM on US reported plan of 'limited strike' on Iran before nuclear talks Global Times 24 Feb 2026 -- In response to a media inquiry regarding reports that the US is considering a limited strike against Iran before nuclear negotiations, Chinese Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Mao Ning said on Tuesday that China is closely watching the developments.
  • Iran Arrests Japan Public Broadcaster's Bureau Chief In Tehran RFE/RL 24 Feb 2026 -- Iran has arrested the bureau chief of Japan's public broadcaster, NHK World, and transferred him to Tehran's notorious Evin Prison, two sources have told RFE/RL's Radio Farda.
  • War risks mount in Middle East ahead of scheduled U.S.-Iran Geneva talks People's Daily 24 Feb 2026 -- While the United States and Iran are scheduled to resume talks on Thursday, there is little indication of de-escalation in their military standoff.

  • Iraqi foreign minister tells US envoy: Government formation 'internal matter' Press TV 24 Feb 2026 -- Iraqi Foreign Minister Fuad Hussein says the establishment of a new government is an "internal matter," weeks after the US threatened to withdraw its support for the West Asian country.

  • President Herzog meets with delegation of U.N. ambassadors Israel MFA 24 Feb 2026 -- President Herzog: "Nations at the U.N. should show moral clarity, which they don't. The weakness of the international system is its lack of ability to fight terror without mercy — not justifying terror and then blaming those who fight against it, like Israel."
  • PM Netanyahu's Remarks to ISA Managers Israel - Prime Minister 24 Feb 2026
  • PM Netanyahu to UN Ambassadors: "Israel is a very small, but very powerful,country, because of technology and science but also because of the spirit of the people." Israel - Prime Minister 24 Feb 2026

  • MOFCOM restricts exports to 40 Japanese entities over military ties; Move a stepped-up response to security policy shift in the country: experts Global Times 24 Feb 2026 -- China on Tuesday put 40 Japanese entities, including Mitsubishi Heavy Industries Shipbuilding Co and SUBARU Corporation, on two lists in light of the growing trend of militarization in the country.
  • China's export controls on certain Japanese entities 'fully justified, reasonable and lawful' to curb Tokyo's 'remilitarization' push: MOFCOM Global Times 24 Feb 2026 -- China's measures to add certain Japanese entities to the watch list and export controls list, announced by China's Ministry of Commerce (MOFCOM) on Tuesday, are aimed at curbing Japan's attempts at "remilitarization" and nuclearization, and are fully justified, reasonable and lawful, a MOFCOM spokesperson said.
  • China adds 20 Japanese entities contributing to military buildup to export control list of dual-use items Global Times 24 Feb 2026 -- China's Ministry of Commerce (MOFCOM) on Tuesday placed 20 Japanese entities, including multiple affiliates of Mitsubishi Corp, on its export control list, banning the export of dual-use items to them over their role in enhancing Japan's military capabilities.
  • MOFCOM places 20 Japanese entities on watch list over unverifiable end users and end uses of dual-use exports Global Times 24 Feb 2026 -- China's Ministry of Commerce (MOFCOM) on Monday placed 20 Japanese entities, including SUBARU Corporation, Mitsubishi Materials Corporation and TDK Corporation, on a watch list after authorities said they were unable to verify the ultimate end users and end uses of exported dual-use items, according to the ministry.

  • "Europe's role today is to stand for the force of rules, not the logic of force" - Alain Berset at UN Council of Europe 24 Feb 2026 -- The Council of Europe's Secretary General, Alain Berset, has concluded his visit to Geneva and Lausanne, Switzerland, which focused on the need to promote democratic security and multilateralism, and to strengthen ties with the United Nations and international bodies, in the delicate context of a breakdown in the international rules-based system.

  • Wider Europe Briefing: The EU's Plan To Counter Russia In Armenia And The Latest On EU Sanctions RFE/RL 24 Feb 2026

  • Somalia: Number of people going hungry nearly doubles in a year UN News 24 Feb 2026 -- The number of people in Somalia facing acute food insecurity has nearly doubled to 6.5 million since last year as hunger levels rise due to worsening drought, conflict and soaring food prices.
